sentence_reviews.text: Graph RAG retrieves entities, relations, communities, and multi-hop paths from an explicit graph before grounding generation or synthesis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The RAG source supports retrieval-augmented generation, but not graph-specific entity/community/path retrieval.
sentence_reviews.notes: Needs a GraphRAG or knowledge-graph RAG source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Graph RAG & structured retrieval is to connect Retrieval-augmented generation, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas to downstream capabilities including Neural search, RAG & question answering, Code models, copilots & repository intelligence, Autonomous research & AI scientist systems.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The cited sources support several underlying concepts, but the downstream capability list is atlas-derived rather than directly evidenced.
sentence_reviews.notes: Treat as a graph-positioning claim; dependencies are plausible but the full downstream list needs direct source support.

sentence_reviews.text: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include Retrieval-augmented generation, Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The stale/private/contradictory/incorrectly ranked limitation is generally plausible for memory and retrieval systems, but most packets cite only broad background sources.
sentence_reviews.notes: The limitation should be narrowed or backed by a memory/retrieval safety source; dependency list portion is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Graph RAG & structured retrieval uses Message passing & neighborhood aggregation as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: The packet references support message_passing or the node mechanism broadly, but the dependency rationale is graph-derived.

edge_reviews.notes: Graph RAG is a variant/use of RAG; “retrieves_from RAG” is the wrong relationship direction/type.
The dependency is central to the node mechanism as described; source support is conceptual rather than exact.
Graph traversal/aggregation may use message-passing ideas, but many Graph RAG systems use symbolic graph retrieval instead.
